seo.title: ProtoPack | Neel
seo.description: A product design case study on creating a backpack that supports hands-on learning, curiosity, and creative exploration.

sections.title: ProtoPack
summary: ProtoPack imagines a backpack as an invitation to learn through making, exploring, and carrying ideas into the real world.
The concept focuses on turning an everyday object into something more tactile, curious, and creatively enabling.

media.alt: Product preview for ProtoPack backpack concept.

items.label: Problem
items.value: Many learning products feel instructional first and inspiring second, leaving little room for curiosity or self-directed exploration.

items.label: Approach
items.value: Explored a backpack concept that supports making, carrying, and interacting with tools in a more playful way.

items.label: Outcome
items.value: The result is a product direction that connects mobility, creativity, and hands-on learning more deliberately.

notes.title: Learning object
notes.text: The backpack was treated as more than storage; it became part of the learning experience itself.

notes.title: Curiosity first
notes.text: The concept aims to make exploration feel inviting instead of overly instructional.

notes.title: Tactile value
notes.text: Physical interaction and form were central to how the idea communicates itself.

sections.label: Context
sections.heading: A learning product becomes more memorable when it invites exploration before explanation
sections.aside: ProtoPack was shaped around the idea that tools can encourage curiosity through form, access, and interaction. The design direction focuses on making the product feel useful, distinctive, and creatively charged.

cards.title:
Define
Carry System
cards.description: The product needed to feel practical in motion while still signaling a more playful purpose.

cards.title:
Support
Quick Access
cards.description: Access patterns were considered so the bag feels interactive, usable, and ready for active making.

cards.title:
Design for
Making
cards.description: The final direction emphasizes a more exploratory relationship between the user, the object, and the act of learning.

metrics.label: Utility
metrics.text: The concept still works as a carry object with clear everyday function.

metrics.label: Access
metrics.text: The product encourages easier interaction with tools and contents.

metrics.label: Curiosity
metrics.text: The form invites exploration and creative engagement.

metrics.label: Identity
metrics.text: The object communicates a stronger point of view than a standard backpack.
